alert_summary: Branch endpoints report application access failures and elevated packet loss.

alert_timeline.message: Service health check failing — no response from endpoint
VLAN adjacency lost on access port
BGP session instability detected — route withdrawals observed
ISP circuit packet loss above 3% threshold

candidate_ranking.reason: WAN handoff is the common upstream dependency for all impacted nodes
On the propagation path between BR-APP-01 and WAN-MPLS-CORE
On the propagation path between BR-APP-01 and WAN-MPLS-CORE
On the propagation path between BR-APP-01 and WAN-MPLS-CORE
First observed symptom — downstream impact evidence points here
Downstream node reachable from root — impacted rather than causal

reasoning_steps: 1. First symptom observed at BR-APP-01 — server reports connectivity degradation.
2. Alert propagation traced upstream: WAN-MPLS-CORE → BR-RTR-01 → BR-FW-01 → BR-SW-01 → BR-APP-01.
3. WAN-MPLS-CORE has highest topological reach — zero or minimal in-degree, maximum out-degree.
4. All impacted nodes (BR-RTR-01, BR-FW-01, BR-SW-01…) are downstream-reachable from WAN-MPLS-CORE.
5. Candidate ranking confirms WAN-MPLS-CORE as primary root cause (score: 0.85).
recommended_actions: Check WAN / MPLS handoff status and verify circuit health
Validate router uplink interface metrics (packet loss, latency, CRC errors)
Review firewall tunnel and NAT policy logs for recent changes
Notify network operations if upstream packet loss continues
